title.rendered: Seven Step Guide to Getting Repairs or Renovations on Your Holiday Home

content.rendered:
Are you one of thousands of Brits who has a holiday homes – from Florida or the Caribbean to Spain or the South of France?

This is the time of year you will either be enjoying the winter sun or booking up flights to get out and open up your property after the winter.

Easter is a popular time for second homeowners to flee the UK, enjoy some sun and get their properties ready for the season and there are some good deals on flights at this time of the year.

If you are getting repairs or renovations done on properties in a foreign country, there are some things to remember to ensure you get the best job possible.

Step 1: Get recomendations

Get a recommendation. You would do this in the UK, so why not in a foreign country? If you know a company or individual has done a good job for others, then you can be much more confident in the job they will do for you.

Step 2: Getting over the language barrier

Get a friend or acquaintance who speaks the language to help out. Sometimes the language barrier could result in miscommunication on key parts of a project, which could lead to you getting something you really didn’t want, and the bill to go with it.

Or perhaps you can find an English speaker or an English company who has set up in the country and runs a business specially for expats. They may be slightly more expensive, but some people prefer the reassurance of better communications.

Step 3: Planning

Book in advance. Good tradespeople are booked up well in advance. If you know you want some work doing to your property and you want to be here to oversee it in the Spring, then get it booked in now.

Step 4: Patients

Be patient. It isn’t the UK and the chances are, things may run on a slightly different timetable to what you are used to.

Step 5: Shop around

Get quotes. Behave as if you would if you were at home and get two or three quotes. There is no harm in checking you are not being over charged and it will also give you a chance to see if you like the person who will be working in your home.

Step 6: Set clear timelines

Ask for clear timelines for completion. While things might not run totally to plan, you need to instruct them when you will next need access to the house so they know when the work needs to be completed by. It won’t harm to put some pressure on and create a sense of urgency.

Step 7: Save money on your international transfers

How do you access your money if you need to do repairs or refurbishments on your property? Withdrawing money directly from your UK account can mean poor rates and unwanted bank charges since Brexit. Many builders don’t accept credit cards. Did you know that you can transfer money from your UK account to your account in any of these countries without charges and to get the best rates.

 

Find out more about international transfers and transferring money online with TravelFX.

https://www.travelfx.co.uk/international-transfers

@graph.name: Travel FX Blog
@graph.description: Travel money news and tips from Travel FX

@graph.name: Lucy Reed
@graph.description: Hi! I’m Lucy, as well as writing blog posts I lead Customer Experience and UX at Travel FX. With over 15 years’ experience in the foreign exchange industry, I specialise in helping UK travellers understand exchange rates, avoid unnecessary fees, and feel confident about their travel money before they fly. Having lived both in the UK and overseas, and travelled extensively over the years, I understand first-hand how important it is to get the details right when you’re planning a trip. From managing your travel budget to choosing the right foreign currency or travel money card, small decisions can make a big difference.
